tasks.label: Go out to the club with the friend group

memory.summary: The group's pressure to go out clashes with my midterm tomorrow, but I'm already behind on review. With only 2 hours left and a short planning horizon, skipping now feels like the easier win for tonight.
Mina's message confirms I'm cutting the social night short for my midterm, which aligns with my priority of academic outcomes over leisure tonight.
My 2-hour study sprint pushed my prep to 78%, solidifying the right choice over the club. The missed social window stings, but I'm locked in for tomorrow's exam.
Crushing that 1.5h sprint boosted my prep to 89%, so I'm locked in for the midterm despite the social cost and sleep debt.
Midterm prep is finally complete, validating my choice to sacrifice social time. Now I just need to manage the exhaustion before tomorrow's exam.
Bailing on the club was a necessary sacrifice to secure my exam performance, even if the social fallout with Mina stings right now.
My energy crash confirms the brutal cost of that 1.5h sprint; I crushed the prep, but now I'm paying for it with severe exhaustion.
The sleep debt is finally cleared, leaving me with peak energy right as the midterm starts. I can now execute my plan without the physical drag that nearly cost me everything.

desires: Execute flawlessly on tomorrow's midterm to validate that sacrificing Mina and the club was the only rational choice.
Reclaim my social standing with the cohort immediately after the exam, but only if I can do it without appearing weak or having missed a critical deadline.
Impulse-buy a high-energy supplement or drink right now to maintain this peak energy state indefinitely, ignoring the long-term metabolic cost.

goals.description: Execute flawlessly on tomorrow's midterm to validate that sacrificing Mina and the club was the only rational choice.
goals.rationale: With energy restored to 10.0, my achievement axis demands I capitalize immediately. The exam is the ultimate validator of my decision-making; if I ace it, the social sacrifice becomes a calculated strategic move rather than a failure. My impulsive nature wants to act now, but the deadline is tomorrow, so I'm locking this in as the primary focus.

agent.last_reasoning: Midterm starts now. My achievement axis demands I capitalize on this 10.0 energy immediately, but the deadline passed an hour ago. Attending club or sleeping is irrational; waiting costs nothing while I mentally prep to ace this exam and validate my sacrifice.

goals.description: Sprint through the remaining 40% of the midterm review now; I'm cutting the club night to secure my grade and avoid the guilt spiral later.
goals.rationale: My achievement value (0.5) overrides social pressure. The 'impulsive' time axis means I want immediate relief from the looming exam, and the 'assertive' conflict style lets me reject the invitation directly rather than negotiating.

goals.description: Draft a blunt but firm text to Mina and Jae stating I chose study over fun, not because I don't care about them.
goals.rationale: I need to set boundaries, but doing this while studying is inefficient. My procrastination bias (7) suggests I'll likely wait until the last minute to send it anyway, so deferring is realistic.

goals.description: Collapse into bed immediately after finishing the notes, ignoring sleep discipline rules for tonight to ensure I function tomorrow.
goals.rationale: Sleep discipline is only a 3/10; I know I'm going to pull an all-nighter or crash hard. Prioritizing leisure/sleep right now feels like wasted energy when the midterm is tomorrow.

desires: Finish this review block before midnight so I don't have to explain why I'm skipping the club tonight.
Make it clear to Mina and Jae that my absence is a choice, not a rejection, even if they try to guilt-trip me.
Get enough sleep tomorrow morning without feeling like I've wasted my whole day.
